text: This is the assembly of this reactor. Step 1, this is the cathode flow plate. It has
laser-painting flow field. Step 2, this is the cathode electrode. The cathode is also
prepared by spray coating carbon dioxide catalysis onto 82363 carbon paper. The catalyst loading
is 3 mg per centimeter square and the active area is 1 by 1. Step 3, this is the cathode
gasket. The gasket thickness is 0.3 micrometer and the ionizer window size is 1 by 1 centimeter.
Step 4, we will place the cathode-side membrane. This is the cathode-side alkaline ion-chloride
membrane. The membrane is sustained on X3750. Step 5, this is the middle air-flight chamber.
The chamber thickness is 3 micrometer and the window size is 1 by 1. The chamber is
used to hold and circulate the liquid electrolytes. The electrolyte is 1 molecule each and it
will be continuously circulated through the middle chamber during operation. Compared
to the previous design, the key change in this reactor is that the middle chamber thickness
is reduced to 3 micrometer. The purpose is to shorten the ion transparent distance, reduce
the organic resistance from the liquid electro-layer and improve the overall and to decrease the
cell voltage. In addition, we induce this reference electrode into the middle layer.
The reference electrode is a mercury oxide electrode which allows us to monitor the electrode
potentials more accurately during operation. Step 6, we add the anode gasket. The anode
gasket is also 0.3 micrometer.
This is the anode side membrane.
Let us put the membrane first into the middle chamber and the gasket.
At the anode side, the membrane is also sustained on X3750.
Okay, under step 8, this is the anode electrode. The anode is also prepared by spray coating
PT-Rosilium carbon catalysis onto L23-C3 carbon paper. The catalysis loading is 2 micrograms
per centimeter square. Step 9, this is the anode flow plate. It also has a 17 flow field.
Finally, after aligning all components, we assemble and tighten the reactor using a torque
of 2 Newton meter.
The reactor is tightened using 4 bolts in a diagonal sequence to ensure uniform compression.
In summary, this reactor keeps the circulating 1 molecule OH liquid electrolytes in the middle
chamber but reduces the chamber thickness to 3 micrometer. Mercury oxide reference electrode
is also added to the middle chamber for more reliable potential monitoring.
This design is intended to improve ionic transport, lower the cell resistance, and provide better
electrochemical diagonal scattering operation.
The torque, using a torque of 2 Newton meter.
Okay, finish this reactor.
